title: wedelia keilii
intro: Wedelia keilii, also known as the Hawaii bleeding heart or Hilo pansy, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pain and combat infections. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable asset in natural remedies. The herbal preparations of Wedelia keilii include tea infusions and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
